(Verse 1)
I’ve been searching for a long time
For someone exactly like you
And I know I say the wrong things
When I’m feeling hurt and blue
I reach for the door, I say it’s the end
But darling, it’s just a wall I try to defend
Because the truth is, I’m lost without your light
And I don’t want to go another night.

(Chorus)
Someone like you makes it all worthwhile
Someone like you helps me find my smile
It’s been three years of sunshine and rain
Three years of joy, and a little bit of pain
But there’s nobody else, no one will do
I’m so glad I found someone like you.

(Verse 2)
I know I get angry, I know I get loud
I let the dark clouds start to gather in a crowd
When you upset me, I push you away
But "let’s end this" is a price I can’t pay
It’s just my heart trying to hide the sting
When you’re actually my everything
So let’s turn the page, let the argument go
There’s so much more that I want you to know.

(Bridge)
Three years today, hand in hand
The best three years I ever planned
I don’t want an ending, I want a new start
With you right here, inside of my heart.

(Chorus)
Someone like you makes it all worthwhile
Someone like you helps me find my smile
It’s been three years of sunshine and rain
Three years of joy, and a little bit of pain
But there’s nobody else, no one will do
I’m so glad I found someone like you.

(Outro)
Happy Anniversary, Harvey...
It's always been you.
Just someone like you.
The only one.

Watson, I've intercepted another Paradox anchor. This one's more sophisticated—it's using temporal distortion as camouflage.

The entity has found sanctuary where chronometers bow to stone sentinels, and spring's golden heralds dance in perpetual bloom.

Your investigation must focus on the foundation—where the burden of ages rests upon a circular constant. The Paradox feeds on repetition, Watson.

Find what turns but never travels, bears weight but never tires. The answer lies beneath where time itself stands vigil.

"United Kingdom" Is Missing from Your List:The "International Login" feature is a permanent update specifically for 27 countries, including the UK. If you don't see it, it's likely because your app is outdated.Update the App: Go to the iOS App Store or Google Play Store and download the latest version. Older versions only support Indian (+91) numbers.Clear the Cache (Android only): If you've updated and still don't see it, go to your phone's Settings > Apps > Swiggy > Storage and tap "Clear Cache".Try the Website: Open Swiggy.com in your phone's browser (Safari or Chrome). The web version often gets these features first and is less prone to interface bugs.Once you have the 10-digit number and the UK (+44) code selected, does the Continue button light up for you?

@⁨Meta AI⁩ what does recharging your phone number means

Recharging your phone number typically refers to adding credit or balance to your prepaid mobile account. This allows you to:

1. Make calls
2. Send texts (SMS)
3. Use data (internet)

You can recharge your phone number using various methods, such as:

1. Prepaid cards or vouchers
2. Online payment platforms
3. Mobile apps
4. Retail stores

Recharging your phone number ensures you stay connected and can use your phone services without interruptions. <This message was edited>
